title: viburnum opulus f. flavum
intro: Viburnum opulus f. flavum, also known as the golden guelder-rose or yellow dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins. The medicinal properties of Viburnum opulus f. flavum have been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy as a medicinal agent.
